pattern: Because moving two positions is free, only a chip's parity matters; gathering everything onto one parity costs one per chip of the other parity, so the answer is the smaller of the even-count and odd-count.
hints: Shifting by two costs nothing, so a chip can reach any same-parity position for free.
The only unavoidable cost is flipping a chip between even and odd positions.
Count how many chips sit on even positions and how many on odd; the answer is the minimum of those two counts.

edge_inputs.description: one chip of the minority parity moves
two odd chips outnumbered are the cheaper side
extreme values but same parity cost nothing

stress.description: random positions up to the value cap, mixed parities
stress.note: wide value range with random parities
